Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/s3-browser/bucket-policy/BucketPolicy.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll analyze each fix before reading the file:

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant` type changed to add `'stateful'` option. The current usages use `ButtonVariant.link`, `ButtonVariant.primary`, and `ButtonVariant.secondary` - all of which are still valid in the new type. No code changes needed - these values still exist.

**Fix 2**: `CodeEditor` was made readonly. This is a type-level change in the library. The usage is just importing and using `CodeEditor` as a JSX component, which works the same whether the export is readonly or mutable. No code changes needed.

**Fix 3**: `CodeEditorProps` base class changed from `Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onChange'>` to `Omit<HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onChange'>`. This is an internal type change in the library. The consumer code just uses `CodeEditor` component - no direct reference to `CodeEditorProps` or its base class. No code changes needed.

Let me read the file to confirm:

Now I can confirm my analysis. Let me review each fix:

**Fix 1**: The `Button.variant` type added `'stateful'` to the union. All current usages use `ButtonVariant.link`, `ButtonVariant.primary`, and `ButtonVariant.secondary` — all of which remain valid in the new type. The type only *added* a new option; no existing values were removed. **No code changes needed.**

**Fix 2**: `CodeEditor` was made `readonly`. This is a library-side type change. The code imports `CodeEditor` and uses it as a JSX component (line 170). Using a readonly export as a JSX component works identically. **No code changes needed.**

**Fix 3**: `CodeEditorProps` base class changed from `Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onChange'>` to `Omit<HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onChange'>`. This is an internal library type change — the consumer doesn't reference `CodeEditorProps` directly, only uses `CodeEditor` as a JSX component. **No code changes needed.**

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `Button.variant` type changed | **Already compatible — no change needed** | All used values (`link`, `primary`, `secondary`) remain valid in the new type. The change only added `'stateful'` as a new option. |
| Fix 2 | `CodeEditor` made readonly | **Already compatible — no change needed** | The readonly modifier is a library-side type constraint. Using `CodeEditor` as a JSX component is unaffected. |
| Fix 3 | `CodeEditorProps` base class changed | **Already compatible — no change needed** | The base class change (`React.HTMLProps` → `HTMLProps`) is internal to the library type definition. Consumer code doesn't reference `CodeEditorProps` directly. |

Rule [family:Modal]:
## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:
  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
  title -> <ModalHeader />
  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  actions -> map to appropriate child component (any)
  bodyAriaLabel -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  bodyAriaRole -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  footer -> ModalFooter (as children, ReactNode)
  hasNoBodyWrapper -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  header -> ModalHeader (as children, ReactNode)
  showClose -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  titleLabel -> map to appropriate child component (string)

Removed children (no longer valid JSX): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Add imports: ModalBody, ModalHeader, ModalFooter from '@patternfly/react-core'

Remove imports (if no longer used): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Deprecated -> v6 migration:
  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core
Matching props:
New props on replacement:
  backdropClassName: string
Removed props (no replacement equivalent): actions, bodyAriaLabel, bodyAriaRole, description, footer, hasNoBodyWrapper, header, help, showClose, title, titleIconVariant, titleLabel

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
